summary: Django REST backend for ForwardLane — 19 apps, dual-Postgres (default + analytical), Redis/Celery, deployed on Railway. The easy_button API (1550 LOC) is the Invesco demo centerpiece with 7 endpoints including NL→SQL. Recent commits show active security hardening (HMAC auth, rate limiting, AllowAny removal), Celery health fixes, and LLM fallback chains (Gemini→Kimi→static). 282 test files exist but easy_button only has 2 test files (permissions + SQL sanitization). No CI pipeline detected (.github/workflows missing).

score_rationale.revenue_potential: Invesco is a major asset manager; demo success → enterprise contract. NL→SQL + signals = differentiated product.
score_rationale.strategic_value: Core platform for ForwardLane's B2B wealth management play. Easy Button is the flagship demo surface.
score_rationale.completeness: API endpoints functional, security hardened recently, LLM fallback chains in place. But missing CI, thin test coverage on critical paths, no enrichment module, customers/invesco has importer but unclear data pipeline health.
score_rationale.urgency: Active demo prep (recent Invesco-tagged commits). Celery health fixes suggest Railway deployment instability. Security commits indicate recent audit response.
score_rationale.effort_remaining: Core API works. Remaining: CI setup, test coverage expansion, Celery stability, monitoring, data pipeline validation.
score_rationale.ci_pipeline: No .github/workflows detected. railway.json exists for deploy but no automated test gating. Commit 8124324 mentions 'CI test gating' but no workflow files found.
score_rationale.test_quality: 282 test files across repo (legacy apps well-tested), but easy_button — the demo-critical app — has only 2 test files (permissions + SQL cleaning). No view/endpoint integration tests.

strengths: Well-structured Django app with clean separation (19 apps, dual-DB architecture)
Security recently hardened: HMAC demo auth, rate limiting, AllowAny removal, SQL injection blocking
LLM fallback chain (Gemini→Kimi→pattern→keyword) for NL→SQL resilience
Comprehensive signal taxonomy (10 signal types with metadata)
Redis caching on meeting prep LLM responses
Railway deployment with health checks and restart policies
risks: No CI pipeline — tests don't gate deploys
easy_button has only 2 test files for 1550 LOC of demo-critical code
Celery health issues (recent fix commits suggest instability)
AllowAny still referenced in some files (user/, core/, forwardlane/urls.py)
No monitoring/alerting beyond Prometheus metrics (recently added)

todo.description: Write tests for all 7 easy_button endpoints: dashboard, clients list/detail, signals list/run, actions, meeting-prep, nl-query. Mock analytical DB and LLM calls.
todo.context: Demo-critical code with 1550 LOC and only 2 test files. Any regression in these endpoints = broken Invesco demo.

todo.description: Create GitHub Actions workflow: run pytest, lint, type-check on PR. Gate merges on test pass. Commit 8124324 claims CI gating but no workflow files exist.
todo.context: Currently zero automated quality gates. Railway deploys directly from branch.

todo.task: Validate Celery worker/beat stability on Railway
todo.description: Recent commits (85117e2, 9cb6c19, 364a2cd) show repeated Celery health fixes. Verify workers stay healthy under load, health endpoint responds, Railway doesn't kill them.
todo.context: 3 consecutive Celery fix commits suggest ongoing instability. Background tasks (signals, ranking) depend on this.

todo.description: Replace hardcoded DB passwords with .env file references. Add .env.example, ensure .env is in .gitignore.
todo.context: docker-compose.yml has plaintext passwords committed to repo.

todo.description: Files still referencing AllowAny: user/resources/user/viewsets.py, user/resources/salesforce/viewsets.py, forwardlane/urls.py, core/views/. Verify each is intentional or replace with proper auth.
todo.context: Commit 6ec8a4c removed AllowAny from easy_button but other apps may still expose unauthenticated endpoints.

todo.description: Run full demo flow: seed analytical DB → hit all easy_button endpoints → verify NL→SQL with sample questions → verify meeting prep LLM responses → check signal run. Document any failures.
todo.context: Individual endpoints may work but full flow hasn't been validated end-to-end.

todo.description: Prometheus metrics exist but no error tracking. Add Sentry SDK for exception capture, especially on LLM fallback failures and DB errors.
todo.context: LLM fallback chains can silently degrade. Need visibility into which provider is actually serving responses.

todo.description: Generate OpenAPI spec for easy_button endpoints. Add drf-spectacular or similar. Critical for Invesco technical evaluation.
todo.context: Views have good docstrings but no machine-readable API spec.

todo.description: Verify rate limiting works under load. Test LLM fallback chain performance. Ensure Redis cache reduces repeat query latency.
todo.context: NL→SQL hits external LLM APIs with 10/min rate limit. Need to verify behavior at limit.

todo.description: Examine customers/invesco/importer/ and tasks.py. Verify data import works, is idempotent, handles errors gracefully.
todo.context: Demo depends on analytical DB having correct data. Import pipeline health is critical but untested.

todo.description: Ensure SECRET_KEY, DEBUG, and all sensitive settings come from env vars (commit 88244cd started this). Audit for any remaining hardcoded secrets.
todo.context: Partially done per recent commits. Need full audit.

todo.description: 1550 LOC file with complex SQL queries and LLM integrations. Type hints would improve maintainability and enable mypy checking.
todo.context: Low urgency but high value for long-term maintenance.

architecture_notes.infra: Railway (web + celery worker + celery beat), Postgres x2, Redis
architecture_notes.recent_focus: Security hardening, Celery stability, NL→SQL feature, Invesco demo prep
